summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on November 9, 2021. The most significant action was accepting Fire Chief and Township Administrator Steve Peter's resignation effective January 1, 2022, appointing him as part-time Township Administrator effective January 2, 2022, and appointing Ed Myers as the new Chief of Fire and EMS effective January 2, 2022. The board also reviewed the preliminary 2022 budget, accepted donations to the police department, and received updates on road construction projects and service department activities.
decisions: Amended agenda to remove items F, G, and H due to Director Holbrook being out sick
Amended agenda to add personnel items B1-B5
Accepted resignation of Fire Chief and Township Administrator Steve Peter effective January 1, 2022
Appointed Steve Peter as part-time Township Administrator effective January 2, 2022
Approved employment agreement between Goshen Township Board of Trustees and Steve Peter for part-time Township Administrator
Appointed Ed Myers as Chief of Fire and EMS effective January 2, 2022
Accepted donation of a $50 Skyline gift card from James Nolder to the police department
Accepted donation of four $25 Skyline gift cards from Ms. Pims Child Care to the police department
action_items: Steve Peter to contact fire and EMS personnel personally about leadership transition before information spreads publicly
Steve Peter and Cheryl to finalize health care renewal quotes and meet with vendors next week
Chad to collect bids for bonus items before final budget numbers come in first Monday of December
Service department to have Christmas lights up prior to November 20th Holly Fair and Light of Goshen parade
Board to conduct detailed fund-by-fund budget review at November 30th meeting
Plan a formal recognition event for new Fire Chief Ed Myers
key_amounts: $200,000+ CDBG grant for Charles Snyder and State Route 28 intersection improvements at zero cost to Goshen
$21,500 received from gov deals sale of old backhoe (previously offered $8,500 trade-in value in 2012)
2022 projected capital projects of approximately $2.2 million
2022 projected starting balance of $2.8 million and ending balance of $1.8 million
$50 Skyline gift card donation from James Nolder
Four $25 Skyline gift cards donation from Ms. Pims Child Care
American Recovery Plan (ARP) stimulus funds partially received with second half expected in 2022
notable_topics: Resignation of Steve Peter as Fire Chief and Township Administrator, transition to part-time Township Administrator
Appointment of Ed Myers as new Chief of Fire and EMS
CDBG grant-funded road construction at Charles Snyder and State Route 28 intersection
Service department restoration of 2006 dump truck and upcoming delivery of new truck
Sale of old backhoe on gov deals for $21,500
Health care renewal process and potential cost increases
American Recovery Plan (ARP) funds and pending federal rule changes
New subdivision development and ODOT-required turn lane at Route 28 and Route 48
Zoning revenue exceeding projections due to growth and construction
Holly Fair and Light of Goshen parade on November 20th
Executive session regarding employment of a public employee
attendees: Ed Myers (Deputy Fire Chief, appointed new Fire Chief)
notes: Steve Peter delivered an extended personal farewell speech recounting his 35-year fire service career across multiple departments (Swarthmore PA, New Jersey, Mason OH, Loveland OH, Xenia OH, and Goshen). He emphasized the importance of family, referenced his wife Molly's lung transplant in 2014, and noted Goshen Fire and EMS grew to 52 employees during his tenure with 19 full-time. Director Holbrook was absent due to illness causing items F, G, and H to be removed. The board entered executive session from 7:10 PM to 8:06 PM before announcing the personnel changes. Trustee Allen was recently re-elected. Meeting adjourned at 8:35 PM.
